reasoning: The question asks for the probability that the daily average temperature in Nice, France on three future dates will be higher than on the reference date of 2025-11-09. Historically, Nice experiences a strong cooling trend from early November through February, with average daily means dropping from around 13.5°C in early November to around 9.5°C by mid-winter. Because of this seasonal trend, the expected temperature differences (Future - Current) are negative, meaning the probability that a future date is warmer than November 9th is less than 50%. The likelihood of a warmer day on 2025-11-16 (1 week out) is smaller because it is close in time and part of the steep autumn cooling phase. Over longer horizons (1 month to 3 months), the natural variability of weather increases forecast uncertainty significantly. Furthermore, explicit instructions dictate that our uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, with probabilities for more distant dates moving closer to 0.5. To respect both the climatological base rates and the mandated uncertainty constraints, I assign progressively higher probabilities (all below 0.5) to the respective dates, drawing closer to the 50% baseline as the horizon stretches into February.
